response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array but for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

module 1: define the task of calculating the sum of absolute differences for each element in the array and specify the condition for calculating the square of the difference

module 2: calculate the sum of absolute differences for each element in the array using the formula for absolute differences and apply the square of the difference for elements at indices that are multiples of 3

module 3: compute the sum of absolute differences for each element in the array and apply the square of the difference for elements at indices that are multiples of 3

module 4: return the final array result with the calculated values for each element in the array

*** 

the final array result is computed by iterating through each element in the original array and applying the appropriate calculation for each element based on its index

the final array result is returned as a list of integers where each element corresponds to the calculated value for the respective index in the original array
